The code uses regular expressions to remove HTML tags and special characters, but it only allows alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and URL encoding. This is insufficient for preventing XSS attacks because an attacker could still inject malicious JavaScript code using certain Unicode characters that are not properly filtered by the regular expression.

One example of such a vulnerability would be an attacker injecting a specially crafted string containing a Unicode character that represents a greater-than symbol (">"). When this string is passed through the function, it may pass the filtering check and cause the browser to execute malicious JavaScript code when the resulting filename is used in a URL or HTML context.

The lack of input validation for non-ASCII characters, combined with the use of URL encoding without proper sanitization, makes this function susceptible to XSS attacks. Therefore, I identify CWE-20 as the most critical vulnerability present in the code.
